rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Create an rcomplex analysis object

Source: R/rcomplex-class.R
rcomplex.Rd

Constructs a container that carries species metadata, networks, orthologs, and analysis results through the rcomplex pipeline. Pipeline functions gain S3 methods that read inputs from the object and store results back, eliminating repetitive argument passing.

rcomplex(
  species,
  traits,
  networks,
  orthologs,
  species_pairs = NULL,
  phylo_pairs = NULL
)

Arguments

species

Character vector of species abbreviations.

traits

Named character vector mapping species to trait labels (e.g., c(BDIS = "annual", BSYL = "perennial")).

networks

Named list of compute_network outputs, keyed by species.

orthologs

Data frame with columns Species1, Species2, hog.

species_pairs

Optional list of length-2 character vectors specifying species pairs for gene-level comparison. Defaults to all pairwise combinations.

phylo_pairs

Optional data frame with columns sp1, sp2 (and optionally pair_name) for module-level comparison.

Value

An S3 object of class "rcomplex".

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
rcx <- rcomplex(
  species = c("SP_A", "SP_B", "SP_C"),
  traits = c(SP_A = "annual", SP_B = "perennial", SP_C = "annual"),
  networks = list(SP_A = net_a, SP_B = net_b, SP_C = net_c),
  orthologs = ortho
)
rcx <- find_coexpressologs(rcx, method = "permutation")
rcx <- find_cliques(rcx)
rcx$edges     # access results directly
rcx$cliques
} # }
